channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Congress Criticises PM Modi's Foreign Policy Amid Iran-US Ceasefire
item.description: The Congress party has sharply criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s foreign policy, focusing on the timing of his Israel visit which occurred just two days before the Israel-US military action ag...

item.title: Dr Zarak Bhat from NIT Srinagar joins AI research hub at University of Edinburgh
item.description: Dr Zarak Bhat, an accomplished scholar from the National Institute of Technology (NIT) Srinagar, has been inducted into the AI hub at the University of Edinburgh, marking a significant milestone in hi...

item.title: London to experience warmer weather than Barcelona with 25C temperatures this Wednesday
item.description: Temperatures in London are forecast to soar to 25C on Wednesday, making the capital hotter than Barcelona, which will only reach 19C. According to the Met Office, the warm weather will extend across m...

item.title: Nearly 400 Gas Stations Closed Across the Philippines Amidst Fuel Price Hikes
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has reported a significant increase in the number of gas stations temporarily closed due to the ongoing challenges posed by rising fuel prices. As of April 8, 2026...

item.title: Retail insolvencies rise 20% amid ongoing economic pressure and weak consumer sentiment
item.description: The number of retail businesses going into insolvency in Australia has surged by 20% in the past year, reflecting growing pressures on businesses and consumers alike. New data from the Australian Secu...

item.title: Peace Arch Hospital Temporarily Diverts Maternity Patients Amid Capacity Pressures
item.description: Peace Arch Hospital in British Columbia has initiated another diversion of its maternity ward due to high patient volumes, a situation affecting the hospital's ability to accommodate incoming labourin...

item.title: Markets rebound as US-Iran two-week ceasefire eases Middle East tensions
item.description: On 8th April 2026, global financial markets reacted strongly to the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran, which eased tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices dropped sha...

item.title: How Designers Use Emotion to Shape Interior Spaces
item.description: Interior design often begins with an emotional vision rather than furniture or color choices. Designers focus on the feelings a space should evoke—calm, warmth, intimacy, or drama—before selecting phy...

item.title: TMC delegation and Election Commission dispute over meeting conduct in Bengal
item.description: A brief meeting between the Trinamool Congress (TMC) delegation and the Election Commission (EC) in Delhi on Wednesday escalated into a dispute over differing accounts of the interaction. EC sources c...

item.title: Minister Sisisi Tolashe Acts Without Legal Authority in Social Development Department
item.description: Since September 2025, the Daily Maverick has closely monitored governance issues at the Department of Social Development under Minister Sisisi Tolashe. A recent letter from President Cyril Ramaphosa, ...

item.title: A-10 Warthog repurposed to engage small boats in Iran amid fleet retention
item.description: The U.S. Air Force's A-10 Warthog has taken on a new maritime role, targeting Iranian boats and coastal threats, extending the aircraft's operational relevance despite ongoing attempts to retire it. T...

item.title: Stan's Lounge: A Stylish New Cocktail Bar at Stanley with Cantonese-Inspired Dishes
item.description: Stan's Lounge, located above the popular Cantonese restaurant Stanley at Howard Smith Wharves, offers a luxurious and atmospheric setting for cocktail lovers. The newly transformed lounge boasts inter...

item.title: Priyanka Gandhi criticises Assam CM Sarma’s remarks on Kharge and asks PM Modi for clarification
item.description: Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi has strongly criticised Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma for making comments she described as ‘rude’ and ‘derogatory’ against Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge....

item.title: Three Possible Scenarios for Reopening the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz has become a critical geopolitical issue, especially after US President Donald Trump's demands for Iran to reopen the vital shipping lane. While it hasn’t been fully closed, shipp...

item.title: Baringo County Struggles With Diesel Shortage Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Drivers in Baringo County, Kenya, are facing a severe diesel shortage, attributed to disruptions in the supply chain linked to tensions in the Middle East, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran. S...

item.title: Intercare's New CEO Aims to Expand Access to Health Care in South Africa and Beyond
item.description: Intercare, a leading South African private health-care provider, has appointed Hendri Hanekom as its new CEO. Hanekom, who has been with the company for nearly 18 years, intends to lead Intercare thro...

item.title: Saskatchewan commemorates Green Shirt Day, encourages organ donor registration in memory of Humboldt Broncos victims
item.description: Saskatchewan is marking the seventh annual Green Shirt Day by urging residents to register as organ donors, honouring the legacy of Logan Boulet. Boulet, a 21-year-old killed in the Humboldt Broncos c...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude AI Experiences Temporary Service Outage Affecting Users
item.description: Claude, the AI chatbot developed by Anthropic, experienced a significant outage, causing disruption for users. The company reported that users encountered an 'elevated rate of errors' while using Sonn...

item.title: Immigration Officers Limited in Assessing Asylum Claims' Credibility
item.description: Canadian immigration experts are raising concerns over the limitations placed on front-line officers who initially interview asylum seekers. Officers from Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada ...

item.title: BYD Faces Scrutiny in Brazil Over Alleged Worker Exploitation
item.description: Chinese carmaker BYD is facing a serious reputational blow in Brazil after being added to a registry of employers accused of subjecting workers to conditions akin to slavery. The listing follows a 202...
